body:
`KernelFunctionOperation` gives users the ability to specify custom kernel functions of `(i, j, k, grid)` as abstract operations. This is useful for a variety of purposes, such as calculating vorticity on the cubed sphere (cannot be expressed in terms of normal abstract operations) or generating random numbers in kernels within the `Field` infrastructure.

```julia
using Oceananigans.Operators: ζ₃ᶠᶠᵃ # called with signature ζ₃ᶠᶠᵃ(i, j, k, grid, u, v)

grid = model.grid
u, v, w = model.velocities

ζ_op = FunctionOperation(Face, Face, Center, ζ₃ᶠᶠᵃ, grid, computed_dependencies=(u, v))

ζ = ComputedField(ζ_op) # identical to `VerticalVorticityField`

barotropic_ζ = AveragedField(ζ_op, dims=3)
```

Resolves #1623 .
title: New `KernelFunctionOperation <: AbstractOperation` for custom kernel functions
